title: Question: verilator xml output
body: --- Author Name: **Rui Terra** Original Redmine Message: 2409 from https://www.veripool.org --- Hi, I am using verilator to generate xml output based on a test code. Here is the code: ``` `define pkg pkg package `pkg; localparam int unsigned NUM_TEST = 2; typedef struct packed { logic vld; logic [1:0] data; } struct_test_t; endpackage : `pkg module m( input var `pkg::struct_test_t [`pkg::NUM_TEST-1:0] i_sign0 , // verilator tag test input var `pkg::struct_test_t [`pkg::NUM_TEST-1:0][`pkg::NUM_TEST-1:0] i_sign1 , // verilator tag test output var logic [2'h1:0][3'h7:0] o_sign0); // verilator tag test endmodule ``` I am a bit confused with the xml output. (I am using the git version. It uses id's) For signal i_sign0 I get the following: (I have 2 basicdtype of size 32) ``` ``` For signal i_sign1 I get the following: (this seems correct to me. Nevertheless the id only matches once.) ``` ``` For signal o_sign0 I get the following: (it only has one packarraytype. but the basicdtype seems to have the 2nd packed size range.) ``` ``` This is confusing to me. I was expecting that o_sign0 had 2 packarraydtype like i_sign1. I am assuming that the o_sign0 basicdtype is not the size of the packed. Is there some error here or I am understanding something wrong? Thanks, Rui Terra
